summary: PR #2235 attempted to fully match ifStatus_802F6194 in src/melee/if/ifstatus.c by replacing a near-matching helper-based linked-list traversal with explicit control flow using locals, labels, and gotos. The PR body says the change was checked locally in objdiff and in decomp.me, but the PR was closed unmerged and had no review discussion in the provided slice.

key_files.reason: Only changed file; contains the removal of the nth_node helper and the rewritten ifStatus_802F6194 implementation.

what_changed: Removed the static inline helper nth_node that walked a linked list by n steps.
Removed the prior near-match comments for ifStatus_802F6194, including the 99.81% match note and decomp.me scratch link.
Rewrote ifStatus_802F6194 to initialize traversal from node->next_gx, then advance through gx_cur->next with explicit gx_head, gx_next, gx_cur, and i locals.
Used labeled blocks advance_node and check_done plus goto statements to express the loop shape directly.
Preserved the early return for node == NULL or n < 0 and retained the redundant-looking second node == NULL branch after that guard, likely because the matched control flow required it.
smart_moves: Targeted the mismatch as a control-flow/codegen issue rather than a semantic issue: the helper version was close, but direct labels better mirrored the compiler’s branch layout.
Inlined the traversal manually instead of relying on a helper, giving more control over loop entry, loop exit, and temporary variable lifetimes.
Separated gx_head, gx_next, and gx_cur temporaries, which makes the intended dataflow of the list traversal explicit and may help reproduce register/liveness behavior.
Kept semantically redundant structure where present in the target pattern instead of over-simplifying the C, a common requirement for binary matching.
Validated the claimed match using both local objdiff and decomp.me according to the PR body.
decomp_lessons: A semantically clean inline helper can still prevent a full match if the compiler’s expected CFG differs; for stubborn near-matches, rewrite the code to match the observed branch structure.
Explicit gotos and labels are acceptable decompilation tools when they recreate the original compiler output more faithfully than a structured for loop or helper call.
Do not remove apparently unreachable or redundant checks automatically during matching work; they can be artifacts of the original source or needed to preserve code shape.

For linked-list traversal matches, pay attention to whether the target advances from a head pointer first, checks the counter first, or checks for NULL first; small ordering differences affect assembly.
Include objective match evidence such as objdiff and decomp.me results in the PR body when submitting a matching-only change.

naming_conventions.name: ifStatus_802F6194
naming_conventions.lesson: Unidentified ifstatus function kept its address-based project naming.

naming_conventions.name: gx_head / gx_next / gx_cur
naming_conventions.lesson: Traversal temporaries were named to reflect head, next, and current HSD_GObj pointers.

naming_conventions.name: advance_node / check_done
naming_conventions.lesson: Goto labels were named after control-flow roles rather than guessed game semantics.

naming_conventions.name: nth_node
naming_conventions.lesson: The removed helper used a generic linked-list traversal name, but abstraction was dropped to satisfy matching.

assembly_or_matching_tactics: Replace helper-based traversal with direct local control flow when a helper produces a persistent near-match.
Use an initial goto into the loop condition block to model a do/while-like or compiler-emitted loop-entry structure.
Split traversal into explicit current and next pointer variables to influence liveness and assignment order.
Return through the same gx_cur variable on both successful index completion and early NULL-list termination.
Retain branch structure that may appear redundant in source if it is needed for the binary match.
review_feedback: No issue comments, review comments, or reviews were present in the provided PR slice.
The PR was closed with merged_at null, so there is no evidence in this slice that the change landed.
follow_up_queries: Find later PRs that matched ifStatus_802F6194 or modified the same traversal in src/melee/if/ifstatus.c.
Search for other removed helper-based near-matches where explicit goto control flow achieved a binary match.
Check whether the same next_gx traversal pattern appears elsewhere in ifstatus or interface code.
